pageHero.title: 12-Week Preparation Roadmap
pageHero.subtitle: Senior/Staff focused. System Design first, coding refresh second, behavioral throughout.

phases.title: Weeks 1-2: System Design Foundations
phases.description: Build the mental framework for distributed systems.

items.text: Review CAP theorem, consistency models, ACID vs BASE
Study load balancing, caching strategies (CDN, Redis, Memcached)
Learn message queues (Kafka, SQS) and event-driven architecture
Start ByteByteGo or Grokking System Design course
Practice: Design a URL shortener (end-to-end)

phases.title: Weeks 3-5: System Design Deep Dive
phases.description: Design real systems. Focus on trade-offs and scale.

items.text: Design a chat system (WhatsApp/Slack scale)
Design a rate limiter and notification system
Design a news feed / timeline (Twitter/Instagram)
Study database sharding, consistent hashing, replication
Design a system with LLM/RAG component (AI-era question)
Practice explaining designs out loud (record yourself)

phases.title: Weeks 6-7: Coding Refresh
phases.description: Patterns over grinding. Focus on medium/hard. 75-100 problems max.

items.text: Complete NeetCode 75 (or Grind 75) - core patterns
Focus areas: graphs, dynamic programming, sliding window, trees
Practice time-boxed solving (25 min per problem)
Review Big-O analysis and space complexity trade-offs
Do 5-10 hard problems to stretch comfort zone

phases.title: Weeks 8-9: Behavioral & Leadership
phases.description: STAR method. Prepare 6-8 stories showing staff-level impact.

items.text: Write 6-8 STAR stories (conflict, leadership, failure, success, deadline, cross-team)
Practice each story out loud (60-90 seconds)
Prepare "why this company" answers for target companies
Practice staff-level narratives: org impact, technical strategy, mentoring
Review common leadership principles (Amazon LPs, Google Googleyness)

phases.title: Weeks 10-11: AI-Aware Practice
phases.description: Learn to use AI tools during interviews that allow them.

items.text: Solve LeetCode problems using Copilot/Claude (simulate AI-enabled interview)
Practice prompting AI for code generation, then verifying/debugging output
Design a system incorporating LLM components (RAG, agents, embeddings)
Learn to spot when AI output is wrong or suboptimal
Practice in CoderPad-like environment with AI assistant

phases.title: Week 12: Mock Interviews & Final Review
phases.description: Simulate the real thing. Get feedback.

items.text: Do 2-3 mock interviews on Pramp or Interviewing.io
Do 1 paid mock with an ex-FAANG interviewer (optional)
Review weak areas identified during mocks
Finalize STAR stories and system design portfolio
Rest and go in confident
